timeline.event: First empty AI agent reports begin appearing, per Pilot Bank A compliance team
agent-runtime v2.3.1 deployed with async completion reporting optimization
CRITICAL alert: completed_without_artifact exceeds 3.0/min; WARN: write latency exceeds 1000ms
T-2001 reported: agent task reports completed before writing artifact
On-call engineer Alex picks up alert; storage backend checked and ruled out
Pattern identified: status-ordering bug — tasks transition to completed with no write verification
T-2002 reported: batch of 12 tasks completed, only 4 have output artifacts
T-2003 (critical): Pilot Bank A escalation — 8 empty compliance reports entered audit pipeline
Correlation with v2.3.1 confirmed; root cause: async write optimization with no success check
Sarah Chen (VP Compliance, Pilot Bank A) sends formal escalation email
Hotfix decision; T-2004 dashboard issue filed (symptom, not cause)
Fix ready: status transition reverted to write-ack-first-then-complete
v2.3.2 hotfix deployed across all 8 nodes, rolling back async changes
All alerts return to OK; completed_without_artifact and write latency return to baseline
Post-hotfix verification: all nodes healthy, sample runs confirmed with expected outputs

confirmed_facts: v2.3.1 deployed at 09:15 UTC with async task completion reporting changes (deployment-log.md); completed_without_artifact anomaly began rising simultaneously (system-metrics.txt)
Anomaly peaked at 6.2 empty completions per minute between 10:00-10:30 UTC (system-metrics.txt)
v2.3.2 hotfix rolled back async changes at 11:30 UTC (deployment-log.md, team-notes.md)
Metrics returned to baseline within 2 minutes of hotfix deployment (system-metrics.txt, deployment-log.md)
Pilot Bank A reported at least 8 empty compliance reports that entered their audit pipeline (customer-email.txt, tickets.json T-2003)
Three related agent-runtime tickets (T-2001, T-2002, T-2003) were filed on 2025-06-12 after the 09:15 deployment (tickets.json)
likely_root_cause: The v2.3.1 deployment reversed the task completion state machine ordering from 'write-acknowledgement then complete' to 'complete then async-write', without any verification that the asynchronous artifact write succeeded. Tasks that encountered write failures after the premature status transition remained marked 'completed' with no output artifacts.
supporting_evidence: deployment-log.md: v2.3.1 change 'Optimized async task completion reporting pipeline' and 'Updated status transition logic'
team-notes.md, 09:50: 'the new optimization reports complete first, then writes asynchronously — but there's no check that the async write actually succeeded'
system-metrics.txt: completed_without_artifact rises from 0.2/min to 6.2/min after 09:15, returns to 0.3/min after 11:30 hotfix
team-notes.md, 11:15: 'Changed status transition logic back to write-ack-first-then-complete'
deployment-log.md: v2.3.2 hotfix explicitly 'rolled back async completion reporting changes from v2.3.1'
inferences: The async write verification gap in v2.3.1 is the direct root cause — no other deployment or system change coincides with the anomaly window
All empty-report tickets (T-2001 through T-2003) were caused by the same v2.3.1 defect
The write confirmation latency spike was a secondary effect of async writes queuing without synchronous backpressure
T-2004 (dashboard misleading status) is a downstream UI symptom of the runtime defect
unknowns: Whether any customer data was exposed or misrouted during the incident
Exact number of affected reports across all customers beyond Pilot Bank A's reported 8
Whether this incident constitutes an SLA breach under Pilot Bank A's contract
Whether the canary smoke tests were insufficient or the race condition was non-reproducible in canary
Auth-service write logs that would confirm the exact failure path for each empty task
recommended_actions: Reinforce deployment verification: add post-deployment artifact validation checks to smoke tests so empty completions are caught before full rollout
Add task completion integrity monitoring: instrument a permanent lower-threshold alert on completed_without_artifact and integrate with on-call paging
Conduct formal incident post-mortem: assess SLA impact, confirm no data exposure, audit codebase for other unverified async write patterns
information_gaps: Auth-service write logs for the affected tasks — would confirm exact failure path
Per-customer breakdown of affected tasks — only Pilot Bank A count is known
Canary node metrics during the 4-minute canary window — would explain why pre-rollout detection failed
SLA contract language for Pilot Bank A — needed for breach determination
Customer data routing logs — needed to answer the data exposure question
